QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_14

question.text: Which of the following losses CANNOT be set off inter-head (i.e., against income from another head)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Long-term capital loss can ONLY be set off against long-term capital gain — it cannot be set off against any other head of income (no inter-head set-off). House property loss (old regime) can be set off against other heads up to Rs.2,00,000. Non-speculative business loss can be set off against any income except salary. Loss from other sources (non-gambling) can be set off against any head. **The Trap:** Capital losses (both LTCL and STCL) are the most restricted — they have NO inter-head set-off at all, only intra-head. Many candidates confuse LTCL with STCL rules; even STCL, while more flexible within capital gains, still cannot go inter-head. **Cross-Exam Context:** Inter-head vs. intra-head set-off rules are a staple across NISM taxation modules. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Capital losses are "trapped" under capital gains. Advise clients to generate sufficient capital gains in the same year or carry forward systematically — don't let capital losses expire after 8 years unused.
